title: New Research Shows Dementia Risk Extends to Spouses
subtitle: A large study suggests that when one partner develops dementia, the other may also face a higher risk.

summary.en: A new study of nearly one million people in Taiwan reveals that spouses of dementia patients are more likely to develop the condition themselves. Researchers suggest this link may be due to shared lifestyles and the stress of caregiving. Experts advise that a diagnosis in one partner should be a signal to check the health of the other. While the findings are significant, doctors emphasize that this is a time for awareness rather than fear. This research adds to recent global efforts to better understand and prevent brain-related diseases.
summary.zh: 一項針對台灣近一百萬人的新研究顯示，失智症患者的配偶罹患該疾病的可能性較高。研究人員認為，這種關聯可能源於共同的生活方式與照護壓力。專家建議，當其中一方確診時，應將其視為檢查另一方健康的訊號。儘管研究結果意義重大，醫師強調此時應提高警覺而非感到恐懼。這項研究為近期全球致力於理解並預防腦部相關疾病的努力增添了重要依據。

paragraphs.en: Following recent updates to global health guidelines on brain health, new research provides a deeper look at how dementia affects not just individuals, but entire households. A large study published in JAMA Network Open has found that people whose spouses have dementia are at a higher risk of developing the condition themselves. This discovery is important because it shifts the focus of dementia prevention from the individual to the couple.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球腦部健康指南近期更新，一項新研究深入探討了失智症不僅影響個人，更會波及整個家庭。發表於《美國醫學會網路開放版期刊》（JAMA Network Open）的一項大型研究發現，配偶患有失智症的人，自身罹患該疾病的風險也較高。這項發現至關重要，因為它將失智症預防的焦點從個人轉移到了伴侶身上。

paragraphs.en: According to MedPage Today, researchers studied nearly one million married people in Taiwan to understand this connection. The study, led by Chi-Shin Wu of the National Health Research Institutes, looked at data from 1998 to 2022. The results showed that both husbands and wives of patients with dementia had a higher chance of being diagnosed with the disease later on. Specifically, the study found about three additional cases of dementia for every 100 spouses over a five-year period compared to those whose partners did not have the condition.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《醫學頁面今日》（MedPage Today）報導，研究人員針對台灣近一百萬名已婚人士進行研究，以了解其中的關聯。這項由國家衛生研究院的吳其炘（Chi-Shin Wu）所領導的研究，分析了從一九九八年至二○二二年的數據。結果顯示，失智症患者的丈夫與妻子，日後被診斷出罹患該疾病的機率皆較高。具體而言，研究發現與伴侶未患病者相比，配偶患病者在五年期間內，每百人約多出三例失智症個案。

paragraphs.en: This study builds on previous research that has examined the health of caregivers. Earlier studies in places like Utah and Korea have also suggested that the partners of people with cognitive disorders face a higher risk of memory problems. However, the new research in Taiwan is notable because it used a much larger group of people, making the findings more reliable. The researchers noted that while younger people showed a higher relative risk, older age groups experienced a greater total number of cases.
paragraphs.zh: 這項研究奠基於先前探討照護者健康的研究之上。過去在猶他州與韓國等地進行的研究也指出，認知障礙患者的伴侶面臨記憶力問題的風險較高。然而，這項在台灣進行的新研究之所以引人注目，是因為其樣本數龐大，使研究結果更具可靠性。研究人員指出，雖然年輕族群顯示出較高的相對風險，但高齡族群的總病例數則較多。

paragraphs.en: Why does this happen? Dr. Wu explained that the connection is likely caused by several overlapping factors. Couples often share the same lifestyle for decades, including similar diets, exercise habits, and environmental exposures. Furthermore, the stress of caring for a partner with dementia can be very high. While the study could not measure the impact of caregiving directly, experts believe the burden of looking after a sick spouse can affect one's own health and well-being. Interestingly, the study also found that having a higher income and more children seemed to offer some protection against this increased risk.
paragraphs.zh: 為什麼會發生這種情況？吳醫師解釋，這種關聯很可能是由多種重疊因素所致。伴侶往往在數十年間共享相同的生活方式，包括相似的飲食、運動習慣以及環境暴露。此外，照顧患有失智症的伴侶所帶來的壓力可能非常巨大。儘管該研究無法直接衡量照護的影響，但專家認為，照料生病配偶的負擔確實會影響個人的健康與福祉。有趣的是，研究也發現擁有較高收入與較多子女，似乎能對這種增加的風險提供一定的保護作用。

paragraphs.en: It is important to note that this research does not mean that every spouse of a dementia patient will eventually develop the disease. Dr. Wu stressed that the main message is one of awareness rather than alarm. He suggested that when a doctor gives a dementia diagnosis to one person, it should be an opportunity to check the health of the other spouse as well. This check-up should include looking at the partner's sleep quality, mood, heart health, and access to support services. By paying attention to the health of both partners, families may be better prepared to manage risks.
paragraphs.zh: 值得注意的是，這項研究並不代表每一位失智症患者的配偶最終都會罹患該疾病。吳醫師強調，主要傳達的訊息是提高警覺而非製造恐慌。他建議，當醫師對其中一人做出失智症診斷時，應將此視為檢查另一位配偶健康的契機。這項檢查應包含評估伴侶的睡眠品質、情緒、心臟健康以及是否獲得支援服務。透過關注雙方的健康，家庭將能更妥善地管理風險。

paragraphs.en: This study is part of a growing body of work aimed at identifying how to protect the brain as we age. Earlier this month, health authorities released new guidelines focusing on lifestyle changes, such as better diet and regular exercise, to lower the risk of dementia. Additionally, other recent research has highlighted how metabolic health—the way our bodies process energy—can influence how quickly the brain ages. Together, these findings suggest that brain health is closely tied to our daily habits and the environments we share with our loved ones.
paragraphs.zh: 這項研究是日益增多的研究工作之一，旨在找出如何保護高齡大腦的方法。本月初，衛生主管機關發布了新的指南，重點在於透過改善飲食與規律運動等生活方式的改變，以降低失智症風險。此外，其他近期研究也強調了代謝健康（即身體處理能量的方式）如何影響大腦老化的速度。綜合這些發現，顯示大腦健康與我們的日常生活習慣，以及我們與摯愛共同生活的環境息息相關。

paragraphs.en: For those living with a partner who has dementia, the findings offer a clear takeaway: do not forget to take care of yourself while supporting your partner. Doctors recommend that spouses stay active, maintain social connections, and seek help when the demands of caregiving become too much. By focusing on overall health, partners can potentially lower their own risks and improve their quality of life. As researchers continue to study these patterns, the hope is that more personalized support will become available for families facing these difficult challenges.
paragraphs.zh: 對於與失智症患者共同生活的伴侶而言，這些發現提供了一個明確的啟示：在支持伴侶的同時，別忘了照顧好自己。醫師建議配偶應保持活躍、維持社交連結，並在照護需求過於沉重時尋求協助。透過專注於整體健康，伴侶們或許能降低自身風險並提升生活品質。隨著研究人員持續探討這些模式，期望未來能為面臨這些艱難挑戰的家庭，提供更個人化的支援。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To use previous work or knowledge as a base for further development.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 基於……之上；以……為基礎進一步發展。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於建築語境，這裡指研究的延續性。文中：This study builds on previous research that has examined the health of caregivers.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A large collection or group of something, such as research or evidence.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指人的身體，這裡指研究的集合。文中：This study is part of a growing body of work aimed at identifying how to protect the brain as we age.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A key fact, point, or idea to be remembered from an event or piece of information.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指外帶食物，這裡指從文章中學到的核心觀念。文中：For those living with a partner who has dementia, the findings offer a clear takeaway: do not forget to take care of yourself while supporting your partner.

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: One Partner's Dementia May Predict the Other's Future Risk

previousArticles.title: New Insights into Dementia: Prevention Guidelines and Household Risks
previousArticles.summaryEn: Global health authorities have released updated guidelines to help people lower their risk of dementia. These recommendations focus on lifestyle changes like exercise and diet. Recent research also suggests that the health of a spouse is closely linked to dementia risk. Experts emphasize that managing individual habits can significantly improve long-term brain health. Doctors are now encouraged to look at the health of both partners when one is diagnosed with the condition.

previousArticles.title: New Research Links Metabolic Health to Faster Brain Aging
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new study has revealed that metabolic syndrome can cause the brain to age faster than normal. Researchers examined over 27,000 people to understand the link between five specific health risk factors and brain health. The findings show that having multiple health issues can make the brain appear several years older than it actually is. Experts suggest that these risks are often manageable through lifestyle changes. This research provides a clearer picture of how physical health impacts long-term cognitive well-being.

quiz.question: According to the study conducted in Taiwan, what specific statistic was observed regarding the spouses of dementia patients over a five-year period?

options.A: There was a 50% increase in dementia diagnoses among spouses.
options.B: There were approximately three additional cases of dementia for every 100 spouses.
options.C: The study found that one out of every three spouses developed dementia.
options.D: The risk was found to be identical to that of the general population.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，研究明確指出「specifically, the study found about three additional cases of dementia for every 100 spouses over a five-year period」。選項 A 誇大了數據；選項 C 錯誤解讀了比例；選項 D 與文章中提到的「higher chance」相矛盾。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between lifestyle and dementia risk based on the provided text?

options.A: Dementia is primarily a genetic condition that lifestyle changes cannot influence.
options.B: The risk of dementia is entirely determined by the environment outside the home.
options.C: Couples who share long-term daily habits may inadvertently share risk factors for brain health.
options.D: High income is the only proven way to completely prevent dementia in married couples.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段提到夫妻長期共享飲食、運動習慣與環境暴露，且第六段強調大腦健康與日常習慣及共同環境密切相關，因此可推論共同的生活方式會影響失智症風險。選項 A 與文章強調生活型態改變的重要性衝突；選項 B 過於絕對；選項 D 錯誤，文章僅提到高收入可能有助於提供保護，並非唯一且絕對的預防方式。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does Dr. Wu mean when he says the main message is one of 'awareness rather than alarm'?

options.A: People should be informed and cautious, but not panicked or overly frightened.
options.B: People should ignore the findings to avoid unnecessary stress.
options.C: The study's results are not serious enough to require medical attention.
options.D: Doctors should provide loud warnings to patients about their future health.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，「awareness」指保持警覺與獲得資訊，「alarm」指恐慌或過度擔憂。Dr. Wu 的意思是這項研究是為了讓大家提高警覺並採取行動，而不是讓民眾陷入恐慌。選項 B 建議忽略研究，與文章本意相反；選項 C 誤解了研究的嚴重性；選項 D 將「alarm」誤解為字面上的警報聲，而非情緒上的恐慌。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding dementia and spouses?

options.A: Dementia is a contagious disease that spreads between spouses through daily contact.
options.B: Caregivers are solely responsible for the health decline of their partners.
options.C: Dementia prevention and health monitoring should be approached as a shared concern for couples.
options.D: The study proves that living with a spouse is the leading cause of cognitive decline.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章的核心主旨在於將失智症的預防與關注點從個人轉向夫妻（couple），並建議醫生在診斷時應同時評估配偶的健康狀況，這顯示了將其視為共同議題的重要性。選項 A 錯誤，失智症非傳染病；選項 B 過度指責照顧者；選項 D 誇大了研究結論，研究僅指出關聯性，而非將其歸咎為主要原因。
